spirits.name: Circle of Spirits
spirits.desc: A druid who hears the unseen voices of the world — an intermediary between the mortal and spirit worlds, calling spirit allies and walking the hidden paths between.

ref.title: Circle Spells
body: Your connection to the spirit world grants Circle spells that are always prepared and don't count against your prepared total: Speak with Animals, Beast Sense, and Detect Magic (3rd level), Augury (5th), Spirit Guardians (7th), and Commune with Nature (9th).

grantsPool.label: Spirit Speaker
grantsPool.reminder: Spirit Speaker: commune with a natural spirit (1 minute) for Advantage on a related Nature, Survival, Insight, or Perception check.

ref.title: Spirit Speaker
body: As an Action, commune with a natural spirit tied to a nearby place, creature, weather phenomenon, or feature. It answers through emotions, images, memories, and brief phrases, within the limits of its nature. Each communion lasts 1 minute and grants Advantage on one related Nature, Survival, Insight, or Perception check. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once), regaining all uses on a Long Rest.
A spirit has also mentored you: you gain Expertise in Nature (or Survival — change it in the builder), or proficiency if you weren't already proficient.

grantsPool.label: Spirit Companion
grantsPool.note: short rest · or trade Wild Shape
grantsPool.reminder: Spirit Companion: manifest an incorporeal spirit ally (within 30 ft, 1 minute) of a chosen form.

ref.title: Spirit Companion
body: As an Action, manifest an incorporeal spirit ally for 1 minute. It occupies no space, can't be targeted or harmed, stays within 30 feet of you, and grants its form's benefits. You have two uses, regaining one on a Short Rest and all on a Long Rest; as a Bonus Action you can trade a Wild Shape use for a Spirit Companion use, or vice versa. You can't change the form while it's active.

Choose one form: Bear (you and allies within 10 ft have Advantage on saves vs. Frightened; grant temp HP equal to your Wis mod each round), Elk (+10 ft speed, ignore natural difficult terrain; let an ally move half its speed without provoking), Fox (Advantage on Stealth; reposition half your speed without provoking), Owl (Darkvision 120 ft — or +60 ft — and Advantage on Perception/Investigation), Raven (Advantage to find hidden creatures and tracks; as a Reaction impose Disadvantage on an attack within 30 ft), Salmon (swim speed and hold breath 1 hour; let an ally ignore difficult terrain), Wolf (a Wisdom-based spirit attack for 1d8 + your Druid level Force damage, slowing the target), or Thunderhawk (a 10-ft-radius Constitution save for 1d6 Thunder and Deafened, scaling to 4d6 at 14th).

grantsPool.label: Between Worlds
grantsPool.reminder: Between Worlds: heightened spiritual awareness for 1 minute — ignore natural difficult terrain, move through plants, see nearby spirits/fey, Advantage to track.

ref.title: Between Worlds
body: As a Bonus Action, enter a state of heightened spiritual awareness for 1 minute: natural difficult terrain doesn't slow you, you can move through nonmagical plants unharmed, you can perceive invisible spirits, fey, and supernatural manifestations within 60 feet, and you have Advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track. You can use this a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all uses on a Long Rest.

grantsPool.label: Spirit Step
grantsPool.reminder: Spirit Step: Bonus Action teleport up to 30 ft, with Advantage on your next d20 and Disadvantage on opportunity attacks against you.

ref.title: Spirit Step
body: As a Bonus Action, briefly pass through the spirit world to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. Until the end of your turn you have Advantage on your next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, and until the start of your next turn opportunity attacks against you are made with Disadvantage. You can use this a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier, regaining all uses on a Long Rest.

grantsPool.label: Spirit Conduit
grantsPool.reminder: Spirit Conduit: a 60-ft aura for 1 minute; each turn choose Ancestors' Guidance, Protection, Wrath, or Revelation.

ref.title: Spirit Conduit
body: As an Action, become a living bridge between the Material and Spirit Worlds: for 1 minute a 60-foot-radius aura surrounds you in which spirits become visible, invisible creatures can't benefit from invisibility, and friendly creatures have Advantage on Wisdom saves and can't be Frightened.

At the start of each of your turns, choose a manifestation — Ancestors' Guidance (one creature gains Advantage on its next attack, check, or save), Protection (one creature gains Resistance to all damage until your next turn), Wrath (one hostile creature makes a Wisdom save vs. your spell save DC, taking 3d8 Force and losing invisibility on a failure, half on a success), or Revelation (one creature can perceive spirits and invisible creatures and gains Advantage on Perception and Insight). Once you use this feature, you must finish a Long Rest to use it again.
